events.name: EVENT:Accepted
events.description: The user has entered data or made a selection then pressed TAB or clicked the mouse to move to another control. This is the event on which you should perform any data input validation code.

events.name: EVENT:AlertKey
events.description: The user pressed an ALRT attribute (or ALERT statement) hot key for an ALRT attribute on the window. This is the event on which you perform the action the user requested by pressing the alert key.

events.name: EVENT:BuildDone
events.description: The BUILD or PACK statement has completed re-building the keys. This is the event on which you perform any build cleanup code. If the user cancelled the BUILD, ERRORCODE 93 is set.

events.name: EVENT:BuildFile
events.description: The BUILD or PACK statement is re-building the file. This is the event on which you inform your user of the progress of the build.

events.name: EVENT:BuildKey
events.description: The BUILD or PACK statement is re-building the key. This is the event on which you inform your user of the progress of the build.

events.name: EVENT:CloseDown
events.description: The application is closing. POSTing this event closes the application. This is the event on which you perform any application cleanup code. Child windows receive EVENT:CloseDown (not EVENT:CloseWindow) when the frame closes.

events.name: EVENT:CloseWindow
events.description: The window is closing. POSTing this event closes the window. This is the event on which you perform any window cleanup code. A CYCLE in this handler prevents the window from closing.

events.name: EVENT:ColumnResize
events.description: On a LIST control with an M in the FORMAT attribute string, the user has resized a column.

events.name: EVENT:Completed
events.description: AcceptAll (non-stop) mode has finished processing all of the window's controls. This is the event on which all data entry validation has run and you can safely write to disk.

events.name: EVENT:Contracted
events.description: On a LIST control with T in the FORMAT string, the user has clicked on a tree contraction box. Use this event to update Level signs and save contracted state.

events.name: EVENT:Contracting
events.description: On a LIST control with T in the FORMAT string, the user clicked on a tree contraction box. If CYCLE is encountered, EVENT:Contracted is not generated and the contraction is aborted. The system is modal during this event.

events.name: EVENT:DDEadvise
events.description: A DDE client has requested continuous updates of a data item from this Clarion server application.

events.name: EVENT:DDEclosed
events.description: A DDE server has terminated the DDE link with this Clarion client application.

events.name: EVENT:DDEdata
events.description: A DDE server has supplied an updated data item to this Clarion client application.

events.name: EVENT:DDEexecute
events.description: A DDE client has executed a DDEEXECUTE statement on this Clarion server application.

events.name: EVENT:DDEpoke
events.description: A DDE client has sent unsolicited data to this Clarion server application.

events.name: EVENT:DDErequest
events.description: A DDE client has requested a data item from this Clarion server application.

events.name: EVENT:Docked
events.description: A dockable toolbox window has been docked or its docked position has been changed.

events.name: EVENT:DoResize
events.description: Functionally equivalent to EVENT:Sized. Used internally by WindowResizeClass and Enhanced Focus support.

events.name: EVENT:Drag
events.description: The user began a valid drag operation and released the mouse button. Posted to the control from which the user is dragging. This is the event on which you pass the dragged data to the drop target.

events.name: EVENT:Dragging
events.description: The user is dragging the mouse from a control with the DRAGID attribute and the cursor is over a valid potential drop target. This is the event on which you can change the cursor to indicate a valid drop target.

events.name: EVENT:Drop
events.description: The user released the mouse button over a valid drop target. Posted to the drop target control. This is the event on which you receive the dragged data.

events.name: EVENT:DroppedDown
events.description: On a LIST or COMBO control with the DROP attribute, the list has dropped. Use this event to hide controls that the drop-list covers.

events.name: EVENT:DroppingDown
events.description: On a LIST or COMBO control with the DROP attribute, the user pressed the down arrow button. This is the event on which you load records when demand-loading the list.

events.name: EVENT:Expanded
events.description: On a LIST control with T in the FORMAT string, the user has clicked on a tree expansion box. Use this event to update Level signs and save expanded state.

events.name: EVENT:Expanding
events.description: On a LIST control with T in the FORMAT string, the user clicked on a tree expansion box. If CYCLE is encountered, EVENT:Expanded is not generated and the expansion is aborted. The system is modal during this event.

events.name: EVENT:GainFocus
events.description: The window is gaining input focus from another thread. This is the event on which you restore any data you saved in EVENT:LoseFocus. The system is modal during this event.

events.name: EVENT:Iconize
events.description: The user is minimizing a window with the IMM attribute. If CYCLE is encountered, EVENT:Iconized is not generated and the action is aborted. The system is modal during this event.

events.name: EVENT:Iconized
events.description: The user has minimized a window with the IMM attribute. This is the event on which you readjust anything that is screen-size-dependent.

events.name: EVENT:Locate
events.description: On a LIST control with the VCR attribute, the user pressed the locator (?) VCR button. This is the event on which you can unhide the locator entry control if it is kept hidden.

events.name: EVENT:LoseFocus
events.description: The window is losing input focus to another thread. This is the event on which you save any data that could be at risk of being changed by another thread. The system is modal during this event.

events.name: EVENT:Maximize
events.description: The user is maximizing a window with the IMM attribute. If CYCLE is encountered, EVENT:Maximized is not generated and the action is aborted. The system is modal during this event.

events.name: EVENT:Maximized
events.description: The user has maximized a window with the IMM attribute. This is the event on which you readjust anything that is screen-size-dependent.

events.name: EVENT:MouseDown
events.description: On a REGION control with the IMM attribute, a synonym for EVENT:Accepted (for code readability).

events.name: EVENT:MouseIn
events.description: On a REGION control with the IMM attribute, the mouse cursor has entered the region.

events.name: EVENT:MouseMove
events.description: On a REGION control with the IMM attribute, the mouse cursor has moved within the region.

events.name: EVENT:MouseOut
events.description: On a REGION control with the IMM attribute, the mouse cursor has left the region.

events.name: EVENT:MouseUp
events.description: On a REGION control with the IMM attribute, the mouse button has been released.

events.name: EVENT:Move
events.description: The user is moving a window with the IMM attribute. If CYCLE is encountered, EVENT:Moved is not generated and the action is aborted. The system is modal during this event.

events.name: EVENT:Moved
events.description: The user has moved a window with the IMM attribute. This is the event on which you readjust anything that is screen-position-dependent.

events.name: EVENT:NestedLoop
events.description: Posted on entry to a nested ACCEPT loop.

events.name: EVENT:NewSelection
events.description: In a LIST, COMBO, SHEET, or SPIN control, the current selection has changed. In an ENTRY control with IMM, generates whenever the contents change or the cursor moves.

events.name: EVENT:Notify
events.description: A NOTIFY statement has been executed. This event is used by the receiver thread to detect that a NOTIFY has been sent.

events.name: EVENT:OpenWindow
events.description: The window is opening. This is the event on which you perform any window initialization code.

events.name: EVENT:PageDown
events.description: On a LIST or COMBO control with the IMM attribute, the user pressed PGDN. This is the event on which you get the next page of records when page-loading the list.

events.name: EVENT:PageUp
events.description: On a LIST or COMBO control with the IMM attribute, the user pressed PGUP. This is the event on which you get the previous page of records when page-loading the list.

events.name: EVENT:PreAlertKey
events.description: The user pressed an ALRT attribute hot key. If CYCLE executes, the normal library action for the keystroke executes before EVENT:AlertKey generates. The system is modal during this event.

events.name: EVENT:Rejected
events.description: The user has entered an invalid value for the entry picture, or an out-of-range number on a SPIN control. Use REJECTCODE() to determine why and PROP:ScreenText to get the user's input.

events.name: EVENT:Restore
events.description: The user is restoring the previous size of a window with the IMM attribute. If CYCLE is encountered, EVENT:Restored is not generated and the action is aborted. The system is modal during this event.

events.name: EVENT:Restored
events.description: The user has restored the previous size of a window with the IMM attribute. This is the event on which you readjust anything that is screen-size-dependent.

events.name: EVENT:Resume
events.description: The window still has input focus and is regaining control from an EVENT:Suspend. The system is modal during this event. (Deprecated since Clarion 6)

events.name: EVENT:ScrollBottom
events.description: On a LIST or COMBO control with the IMM attribute, the user pressed CTRL+PGDN. This is the event on which you get the last page of records when page-loading the list.

events.name: EVENT:ScrollDown
events.description: On a LIST or COMBO control with the IMM attribute, the user attempted to move the highlight bar down. This is the event on which you get the next record when page-loading the list.

events.name: EVENT:ScrollDrag
events.description: On a LIST or COMBO control with the IMM attribute, the user moved the scroll bar thumb and released the mouse button. Use PROP:VScrollPos to determine the target position.

events.name: EVENT:ScrollTop
events.description: On a LIST or COMBO control with the IMM attribute, the user pressed CTRL+PGUP. This is the event on which you get the first page of records when page-loading the list.

events.name: EVENT:ScrollTrack
events.description: On a LIST or COMBO control with the IMM attribute, the user is currently moving the scroll bar thumb. Use PROP:VScrollPos to dynamically scroll the displayed records.

events.name: EVENT:ScrollUp
events.description: On a LIST or COMBO control with the IMM attribute, the user attempted to move the highlight bar up. This is the event on which you get the previous record when page-loading the list.

events.name: EVENT:Selected
events.description: The control has received input focus. This is the event on which you should perform any data initialization code.

events.name: EVENT:Selecting
events.description: Modal event executed before placing EVENT:Selected in the window's event queue. If CYCLE is encountered, EVENT:Selected is not generated. Use FIELD() to get the equate label of the field about to be selected.

events.name: EVENT:Size
events.description: The user is resizing a window with the IMM attribute. If CYCLE is encountered, EVENT:Sized is not generated and the action is aborted. The system is modal during this event.

events.name: EVENT:Sized
events.description: The user has resized a window with the IMM attribute. This is the event on which you readjust anything that is screen-size-dependent.

events.name: EVENT:Suspend
events.description: The window still has input focus but is giving control to another thread to process timer events. The system is modal during this event. (Deprecated since Clarion 6)

events.name: EVENT:TabChanging
events.description: On a SHEET control, focus is about to pass to another tab. This is the event on which you perform any necessary housekeeping code for the tab you are leaving.

events.name: EVENT:Timer
events.description: The TIMER attribute has triggered. This is the event on which you perform any timed actions, such as clock display or background record processing for reports or batch processes.

events.name: EVENT:Undocked
events.description: A dockable toolbox window has been undocked.
